body:
## Context
`src/cart.js` exposes `computeTotal(items)`, which maps each item to `price * qty` and then calls `Array.prototype.reduce((a,b) => a+b)` with **no initial value**.

## Bug
On an empty cart (`computeTotal([])`), `reduce` with no initial value throws `TypeError: Reduce of empty array with no initial value`. (In other JS contexts this manifests as a `NaN` total.) Either way, an empty cart does not produce a correct total of 0.

## Goal
`computeTotal([])` must return `0`, and non-empty carts must continue to total correctly (e.g. `[{price:2,qty:3},{price:5,qty:1}]` -> `11`).

## Acceptance criteria
- `computeTotal([])` returns `0`.
- Existing non-empty behavior unchanged.
- An automated test in `test.js` covers the empty-cart case and passes via `node test.js`.
title: Cart total shows NaN/throws on an empty cart
